executed_items.next_blocker: PG245 Twinflame Tyrant / Verge Rangers precheck cannot run while PostgreSQL target has no response; remaining runtime cards require split-scope or manual mapper/test-scenario work.
Big Score already failed exact hand-filter gates; Esper Sentinel, Monument to Endurance, Rise of the Eldrazi, and other cuts remain core/high-exposure.

The only high-scoring tutor cuts are protected engine cards such as Land Tax, Library of Leng, Scroll Rack, and Sensei's Divining Top; Thor and Creative Technique tutor cuts have prior strong-seed regressions.
Squee route is modeled only when accessed; Hidden Retreat remains a read-only overlay until approved PG apply/sync, and all current access cuts are cross-lane, protected, or prior-blocked.

recommended_next_actions: Do not create a blind Lorehold deck swap from the current queue.
When PostgreSQL responds, rerun PG245 precheck for Twinflame Tyrant / Verge Rangers and only then consider apply/postcheck/sync.
Separately prepare PG244 Hidden Retreat precheck/apply/sync if the access-density path is chosen.
If PostgreSQL remains unavailable, implement one split-scope runtime family or a manual mapper/test scenario from the 61-card runtime gap queue before generating new deck packages.
A new candidate deck change requires a new non-core cut source; current hand-filter, tutor, and Squee/access cut models do not expose one.
